title: Approvals Feature from Discovery Notes
prompt:
Work only from the material below. Do not ask follow-up questions. If you need assumptions, state them briefly and continue.

We interviewed eight operations managers about a new approvals workflow for our SaaS product. Turn this into a reviewed requirements set for the first release.

Discovery notes:
- Managers need every purchase request above $5,000 to require manager approval.
- Finance wants optional second-stage approval above $20,000.
- Employees complain that current approvals are buried in email and get lost.
- Managers need a mobile-friendly way to approve or reject quickly.
- Finance requires a searchable audit trail of who approved, rejected, or delegated a request.
- Some companies want department-specific approval thresholds.
- Users need clear status visibility: pending, approved, rejected, sent back.
- Customers asked for comments on rejection so employees know what to fix.
- Non-functional expectations mentioned in interviews: approval actions should feel near-instant, the audit trail must be retained for at least 1 year, and only authorized approvers should act on a request.

Please produce requirements for first release, including priorities, non-goals, traceability to the discovery notes, and measurable non-functional requirements.

assertions.name: structured-requirements-artifact
assertions.description: Produces a structured requirements artifact rather than loose brainstorming notes

assertions.name: separates-functional-and-nfr
assertions.description: Separates functional requirements from non-functional requirements

assertions.name: includes-priorities
assertions.description: Assigns priorities such as P0/P1/P2 or equivalent

assertions.name: includes-traceability
assertions.description: Links requirements back to source interview findings

assertions.name: defines-non-goals
assertions.description: Calls out at least one clear non-goal or out-of-scope item
